title: Serialize and Deserialize a Binary Tree
description:
Given Design algorithms to serialize a binary tree to a string and deserialize the string back to the identical tree. Use a clear format that handles nulls.

    

Constraints:
    - 0 ≤ nodes ≤ 10^5
- Values are integers
explanation:
Intuition
A preorder traversal with sentinels captures both structure and values. Deserialization consumes tokens in the same order to rebuild nodes.

Approach
1. Initial Setup: Serialize by preorder appending '#' on None
2. Main Algorithm: Split string and build recursively
3. Return Result: Return the rebuilt root

Complexity Analysis
- Time Complexity: O(n)
- Space Complexity: O(n) output + O(h) recursion

Key Insights
- Traversal with null markers uniquely identifies the tree
- Using an iterator avoids global indices

Common Pitfalls
- Omitting nulls makes decoding ambiguous
- Forgetting to consume tokens in order breaks reconstruction
